The '''almost''' aspect (also called '''proximative''') in Aelaki expresses that an action or state nearly occurred but did not. It is one of the [[Stative aspects|stative verb aspects]], formed with the prefix '''ko-''' (or '''ke-''' with umlaut stems).

== Formation ==

The almost aspect is formed by prefixing '''ko-''' to the stative verb stem.

{| class="wikitable"
! Stative verb !! Almost !! Meaning
|-
| zoduk (know) || kozoduk || almost learn (nearly start to know)
|-
| apafath (fall) || ko'apafath || almost fall
|}

== Continuative form ==

The continuative of the almost aspect is formed by reduplicating the prefix:

{| class="wikitable"
! Base !! Continuative !! Meaning
|-
| kozoduk || kokozoduk || almost learning (in the process of nearly knowing)
|}

== Semantic scope ==

The almost aspect indicates that a state came close to being realized but ultimately was not. With ''zoduk'' "know", the almost form ''kozoduk'' means "almost learn" -- that is, to come close to knowing something without fully achieving it.

== Relationship to other stative aspects ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Prefix !! Aspect !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| ho- || [[Probable]] || hozoduk || curious (about to know)
|-
| nü- || [[Inchoative]] || nüzoduk || learn (start to know)
|-
| nï- (+ umlaut) || [[Cessative]] || nïzedik || forget (stop knowing)
|-
| lu- || [[Resumptive]] || luzedik || remember (resume knowing)
|-
| '''ko-''' || '''Almost''' || '''kozoduk''' || '''almost learn'''
|}

== See also ==

* [[Inchoative]]
* [[Cessative]]
* [[Resumptive]]

[[Category:grammatical aspects]]
[[Category:Stative aspects]]
